methods.description: The geochemical organic characterization (Total Organic Carbon-TOC) expresses the amount of organic matter contained in the sediment. This method is used to understand the interaction of input, preservation, and dilution of organic matter in recent sediments, which is crucial for quantifying critical parameters accurately.

steps.description: Collect T2 core samples from Mina Modelo at various depths.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core samples from specified depths

steps.description: Measure TOC and TS contents in the collected samples.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze TOC and TS contents in sediment samples

steps.description: Interpret TOC and TS values to infer depositional environment conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret TOC and TS data to assess depositional conditions

methods.description: This method uses statistical Q-mode analysis with Ward's method and Euclidean distance to identify associations of samples, which are related to palynofacies defined by cluster R-mode.

steps.description: Perform Q-mode hierarchical cluster analysis using Ward's method with Euclidean distance.
Identify and interpret four main associations of samples.

methods.method_name: Palaeoenvironmental Characterization Based on Intervals Generated by Cluster Analysis
methods.description: This method uses the associations generated by cluster analysis to subdivide the sedimentary section into intervals, aiming to infer hydrological fluctuations over time.

steps.description: Subdivide the sedimentary section into intervals based on cluster analysis results.
Estimate pond water level based on the frequency of Botryococcus and other algae.

steps.description: Interpret intervals to infer hydrological fluctuations and environmental conditions over time.
